feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Column | This exercise ‘sweet spot’ is linked to greater longevity
entry.summary: A new study pinpoints how many minutes per week of strength training might help people avoid death from conditions including heart attacks and Alzheimer’s.

entry.title: Covid vaccine linked to broad protections against heart conditions, study finds
entry.summary: The vaccine reduced cardiovascular conditions, hospitalizations and deaths of all causes, including those not linked to covid, researchers said.

entry.title: 5 Commonly Missed Signs Of Bowel Cancer
entry.summary: What if your symptoms are more than just stress or IBS?

entry.title: Column | The dirty secret in your soap: Antibacterial soap may be breeding superbugs
entry.summary: Don't fall for hygiene theater: Antibacterial products aren't better than regular soap and water.

entry.title: Ozempic was found in a 2025 clinical trial to reduce measurable biological age in adults by approximately 3 to 5 years — with improvements in inflammation, cardiovascular health, and kidney function — meaning a drug originally designed to manage blood s
entry.summary: The drug currently sweeping through the world’s prescription markets as a treatment for diabetes and obesity may turn out to do something its manufacturer never quite designed it for. In July 2025, a research team led by Michael J. Corley of UC San Diego, wit…

entry.title: Think higher SPF means better? Experts say not always
entry.summary: These are the common mistakes dermatologists want you to stop making when it comes to sunscreen, especially for people over 40.

entry.title: Women sleep better than men – but experience the opposite
entry.summary: Women are more likely than men to report poor sleep, even though objective measurements show that their sleep is better in several respects, according to new research from Karolinska Institutet.

entry.title: Monmouthshire mum says she may have avoided ovarian cancer if she lived in England
entry.summary: Heather Morgan's postcode meant she was ineligible for a test that could have revealed her risk.

entry.title: Scientist confronting the rising global threat of mosquitoes
entry.summary: Growing up in Tahiti, Anna-Bella Failloux saw first-hand the threat posed by mosquitoes: nearly a third of adults on the picturesque island once had swollen...

entry.title: RFK Jr. Orders American Exposed to Hantavirus to Stay Quarantined Against Medical Advice
entry.summary: Health secretary says cruise passenger Angela Perryman has to stay isolated in Nebraska despite CDC report advising otherwise

entry.title: The Laundry Room Staple Sparking Cancer Concerns Online — and What Experts Actually Say
entry.summary: Experts break down the research behind viral claims linking dryer sheets and fabric softeners to cancer

entry.title: Popular 'Ice' Vape Flavors May Have a Hidden Effect on Your Heart
entry.summary: For years, flavour additives in e-cigarettes have been largely viewed as a secondary concern compared with nicotine.

entry.title: American doctor who recovered from Ebola arrives back in U.S., says he's "feeling well"
entry.summary: Dr. Peter Stafford, his wife, Rebekah Stafford, and their four children all arrived safely on Monday, according to Serge, a Pennsylvania-based Christian missions organization.

entry.title: A 'Useless' Organ That Doctors Often Remove May Actually Fight Cancer
entry.summary: There's a small fatty gland that sits behind your sternum and is often said to be 'useless' in adulthood.

entry.title: Teen Hospitalized for Flesh-Eating Bacteria After Swimming with Siblings in Florida
entry.summary: Joziah Thompson “is currently fighting for his life” after contracting Vibrio vulnificus

entry.title: Slow breathing can influence brain activity and decision behavior
entry.summary: A new study from the German Institute of Human Nutrition Potsdam–Rehbruecke (DIfE) and Charité—Universitätsmedizin Berlin shows for the first time that targeted control of human breathing rhythm can influence decision behavior by modulating heart and brain fu…

entry.title: This 5-Second Pinky Exercise To Reduce Dementia Is Going Viral — And Neurologists Have Thoughts
entry.summary: We asked neurologists if TikTokers are onto something.

entry.title: Researchers identified a personality profile that combines high empathy with high levels of narcissism, Machiavellianism, and psychopathy, in a finding that has complicated the standard scientific picture of empathy as an unambiguously prosocial human trait
entry.summary: The popular assumption about empathy, repeated across most of contemporary psychology, ethics, education, and public discourse, is that it is one of the unambiguously good human capacities. The empathic person feels what other people feel, understands their s…

entry.title: Self-pleasure before bed is linked to falling asleep faster and sleeping better
entry.summary: Engaging in self-pleasure before going to sleep is linked to falling asleep faster, experiencing better physical rest, and waking up with elevated positive emotions, according to a recent survey exploring the hidden boundaries between waking behavior and drea…

entry.title: New Hampshire man hospitalized for weeks after contracting tick-borne illness
entry.summary: A Concord man has become seriously ill after contracting a tick-borne illness.

entry.title: COVID vaccines still protect against heart problems, large study finds
entry.summary: Despite continued benefits, anti-vaccine rhetoric has driven down vaccination.

entry.title: At-home brain implant gives man with motor neuron disease his daily life back
entry.summary: The device has helped a man with motor neuron disease communicate and control his computer for nearly two years.

entry.title: The CDC Found a Pregnancy Risk More Americans Appear Willing to Take
entry.summary: There's no safe amount of alcohol use during pregnancy, health experts widely agree.

entry.title: 20 Healthy Recipes to Help Lower Your Dementia Risk
entry.summary: Try this list of meals and snacks that follow the MIND diet—an eating pattern that can help reduce your risk of dementia—to enjoy at any time of day.

entry.title: Measles case detected at SFO days before World Cup start
entry.summary: People at SFO and a San Jose Trader Joe's may have been exposed to the measles virus.

entry.title: Why Some People Are Wired to Wake Up Early, According to Experts
entry.summary: Ever wonder why some people wake up early without an alarm? Experts explain how genetics, circadian rhythms, and daily habits influence your sleep schedule.

entry.title: The latest benefit of obesity drugs: boosting testosterone and sperm quality
entry.summary: Preliminary study finds that testosterone levels increase or stabilize in people taking GLP-1 medications.

entry.title: DPH confirms first case of measles in Connecticut this year
entry.summary: The Connecticut Department of Public Health has confirmed the state’s first case of measles this year in an unvaccinated adult from Hartford County…

entry.title: Connecticut Confirms First Measles Case Of 2026
entry.summary: Health officials on Monday said the case was in an unvaccinated resident who recently traveled internationally.

entry.title: The marshmallow test, redone with ten times as many children, found that a four-year-old's willpower mostly stopped predicting teenage success once family background was taken into account
entry.summary: The famous experiment seemed to show that a child who could resist one treat for two grew up to do better in life. A 2018 conceptual replication with a larger, more representative sample found a much weaker link, most of it explained by the home a child came …

entry.title: Diagnostic dilemma: Brain scans following a man's hospital visit for leg weakness revealed a surprising finding
entry.summary: A man went to hospital complaining about weakness in his left leg, and subsequent brain scans revealed his abnormally small brain.

entry.title: Stray cat attacks 5 people, puppy in Polk County, tests positive for rabies
entry.summary: A cat has tested positive for rabies after it attacked multiple people in Polk County, officials said.

entry.title: Column | As a doctor, I tell people to do these 4 things to reduce age-related muscle loss
entry.summary: Resistance training, protein and recovery remain the most powerful tools for preserving strength and independence later in life.

entry.title: Dementia: Joint pain supplement may speed progression
entry.summary: Glucosamine, a widely used joint pain supplement, may speed the progression of dementia and even increase mortality, according to a new study.

entry.title: Copper drug restores memory and clears toxic Alzheimer's proteins, preclinical study finds
entry.summary: Monash University researchers have found in laboratory experiments that a drug that delivers copper to the brain significantly reduces toxic Alzheimer's proteins and improves long-term spatial memory.

entry.title: 5 Daily Exercises That Reverse Aging Better Than Gym Workouts After 60
entry.summary: A wellness coach shares five daily exercises that support strength, balance, and independence after 60, no gym needed.

entry.title: Single-cell RNA sequencing of terminal ileal biopsies identifies signatures of Crohn’s disease pathogenesis
entry.summary: IBDverse is a single-cell RNA atlas of terminal ileal biopsies comprising 1.1 million cells from 111 patients with Crohn’s disease and 232 healthy participants. Analysis of the data identifies genes, pathways and cell populations associated with the disease a…

entry.title: Who you live with has a major impact on your gut health — even if you have different diets
entry.summary: People who lived together shared 26% of the same oral microbes, a new study showed. Here’s why (and it’s not about kissing).

entry.title: The Best Breakfast to Eat If You're Trying to Lower Your LDL Cholesterol
entry.summary: Banana overnight oats support healthy LDL cholesterol with soluble fiber, unsaturated fats, and potassium. Plus, expert-backed tips for better cholesterol.

entry.title: Why Are Ultra-Processed Foods So Bad for You? Study Offers Insight
entry.summary: A recent study found that it's not just the low nutrient profile of ultra-processed foods that causes negative health effects, but also the processes used to make them. Here's why they can be so harmful to health.

entry.title: Experimental treatment kills prostate tumor cells while reawakening antitumor immunity
entry.summary: Cornell Prime dots – known as C’ dots – are effective against prostate tumors, according to a new preclinical study led by investigators at Weill Cornell Medicine and the Cornell Duffield College of Engineering.

entry.title: Mom of three diagnosed with cancer after doctors claimed she was ‘too fit and young’ to be ill
entry.summary: The 37-year-old, from Stratford-upon-Avon, Warks., was only sent for urgent tests two years later after a female doctor realised “something wasn’t right”. Lydia was referred to Warwick Hospital in …

entry.title: The Link Between Vitamin C And Brain Health Just Got Even Clearer
entry.summary: We can't make our own vitamin C, so we need to imbibe enough of this essential nutrient through our diets.

entry.title: Another possible benefit of GLP-1s? Improved male fertility
entry.summary: Not only are GLP-1 medications likely not harmful to male fertility, but they may help improve testosterone levels, sperm health and production, study says.

entry.title: Californian Contracts Fatal Disease 'Breeding Wild Rats'
entry.summary: Berkeley confirms first leptospirosis death in more than a decade

entry.title: 6 Hobbies That May Help Slow Brain Aging as You Get Older
entry.summary: Can hobbies slow brain aging? Learn how dance, music, puzzles, art, birdwatching, and gardening may support memory and thinking.

entry.title: Millions take calcium and vitamin D for stronger bones. A major review finds little benefit
entry.summary: For years, calcium and vitamin D supplements have been promoted as a simple way for older adults to protect their bones and prevent falls. But a massive review of nearly 154,000 people found that calcium, vitamin D, or a combination of both provided little to…

entry.title: Why your blood sugar may rise in summer even when you're eating 'Healthy,' and the smart swaps that can help
entry.summary: Summer has a way of changing what ends up on the plate. The soaring temperatures make cold drinks, juicy fruits, sweet lassis and refreshing sherbets almost irresistible. They feel harmless, even healthy. After all, they're often homemade and made from natura…

entry.title: The Surprising High-Protein Ingredient You Should Be Adding to Your Smoothie—but Probably Aren’t
entry.summary: Silken tofu is an incredibly versatile ingredient to add to your smoothies for a big boost in nutrition, plant-based protein and an ultra-creamy texture that works in any smoothie recipe.

entry.title: We’re not as helpless against dementia as we think
entry.summary: US dementia cases are projected to double by 2060. But age-specific risk has fallen about 13 percent per decade — and up to 45 percent of dementia may be preventable.
